reviews.review_msg: agree 100% with below comment. Absolutely hated this class! Class has been over for 3 weeks and she has yet to hand back assignments from November. Would not recommend her to anyone.

Totally unimpressed with her. I can guarantee that if you take a class with her, she WILL turn up late for majority of the classes (15-25 minutes late) and you won't get assignments back in a timely manner (not to mention that her marking style makes no sense whatsoever). Classes are an easy GPA booster, but she really should not be teaching.

Many students struggled with the course due to a lack of sociological imagination. Thus, having a good understanding on sociology was highly recommended! All in all, it was a good course, and she was really helpful!

The course was on religion, gender and the global south. While Ms. Zakiuddin is a very articulate speaker her approach is postmodern and most topics got bogged down in this incomprehensible, relativist jargon.  There is not much room, in general, for opposing viewpoints, particularly on religion, and specifically Islam.
This class was a demonstration of the professionalization of the Humanities, where relativism substitutes for objective analysis, content is exhausted but salaries need to be made.
